description:
EXTENDED (v2) crypto golden vectors for the offline-package PackageVerifier.verifyExtendedSignature (T17), consumed field-for-field by BOTH the Android JVM test and the iOS test (NFR2). Generated by scripts/gen-verify-extended-fixture.sh with OpenSSL's native Ed25519 (NFR1: no third-party crypto). The canonical v2 payload that is signed/verified is UTF8(sigV2Tag + '
' + version + '
' + digest + '
' + minAppVersion + '
' + fileManifestHash + '
' + rollbackFloor) with sigV2Tag='offline-package-sig-v2' — identical in PackageVerifier.kt, PackageVerifier.swift and the generator. The v2 tag domain-separates this from the v1 (version+digest) scheme so a legacy v1 signature is structurally invalid here (the legacyV1Signature case pins that anti-bypass red line). publicKeys maps keyId -> raw 32-byte Ed25519 public key (base64). The private key is ephemeral and intentionally discarded — these are throwaway TEST vectors. Each case is self-describing: presented* fields are what the verifier is HANDED (a tampered field differs from what was signed); expectSignatureValid says whether verifyExtendedSignature must accept; both ends MUST agree. The validManifestEmptyTail case is the EMPTY-TAIL shape (fileManifestHash='' and rollbackFloor='') that the live app-manifest fetch actually signs/verifies — handoff D1 promotes it to the v2 contract's primary shape; its signature is a REAL OpenSSL signature over the empty-tail canonical payload (two trailing newlines).
canonicalPayload:
UTF8(sigV2Tag + '
' + version + '
' + digest + '
' + minAppVersion + '
' + fileManifestHash + '
' + rollbackFloor)

cases.name: validManifestEmptyTail
cases.comment:
EMPTY-TAIL PRIMARY SHAPE (handoff D1): same version/digest/content/minAppVersion as the valid case, but fileManifestHash='' and rollbackFloor='' — exactly what the live app-manifest fetch hands the verifier (OfflinePackageManager.kt:466-467 / iOS twin pass two empty strings). The signature is a REAL OpenSSL Ed25519 signature by the SAME key over the empty-tail canonical payload UTF8(tag + '
' + version + '
' + digest + '
' + minAppVersion + '
' + '' + '
' + '') (two trailing newlines), so verifyExtendedSignature MUST accept: expectSignatureValid=true.

cases.name: embeddedNewlineField
cases.comment:
NON-INJECTIVE-ENCODING GUARD: minAppVersion carries an embedded newline ('3.2.0
9f...M'). Pure-newline field joining is not injective, so without a guard this tuple could re-split into a DIFFERENT tuple with the SAME payload bytes and smuggle a lower rollbackFloor past the signature. The payload builder REJECTS any newline-bearing v2 field fail-closed (Kotlin MalformedInputException / Swift .malformedInput) BEFORE the signature is checked, so verifyExtendedSignature must NOT accept this — expectSignatureValid=false. The signature is borrowed from the valid case (irrelevant: rejection happens before it is verified). No legal field ever contains a newline, so this only refuses adversarial input.
